title: Wire Two Containers Together
description:
## Connect Containers on a User-Defined Network

**Requirements:**
1. Create a user-defined bridge network named **`appnet`**
2. Run an **`alpine`** container named **`db`** attached to `appnet` (keep it alive, e.g. `sleep 3600`)
3. Run an **`alpine`** container named **`app`** attached to `appnet` (also kept alive)

On a user-defined network, `app` and `db` can resolve each other by name.

> 💡 `docker network create appnet`, then `docker run -d --name db --network appnet alpine sleep 3600` (and similarly for `app`).

hints.title: Create the network
hints.body: A user-defined bridge network provides automatic DNS between attached containers.

hints.title: Attach containers at run time
hints.body: Use --network to attach each container to appnet as it starts.
hints.command: docker run -d --name db --network appnet alpine sleep 3600 && docker run -d --name app --network appnet alpine sleep 3600

hints.title: Verify connectivity by name
hints.body: From app, ping db by its container name to confirm DNS works on the shared network.

validation.description: Checks that 'appnet' exists and both 'db' and 'app' are attached to it.
